operationalSituations.description: Normal vehicle operation — all zone controllers reachable
Partial network fault — one or more zone controllers unreachable
Safety-critical manoeuvre — emergency braking or collision avoidance active
HPC software fault — runaway process, crash, or OOM condition
Elevated network latency — congestion, EMI, or hardware degradation
Adversarial access — attacker present on the zone Ethernet bus

hazards.description: Loss of command delivery to safety-critical zone (e.g. braking actuator)
Spurious command sent to wrong zone controller
Zone controller watchdog not kicked, leading to unintended reset
Delayed command delivery — zone responds within protocol timeout but too slowly for real-time control
Corrupted command payload — bit error or memory fault causes wrong actuator value to be applied
Zone controller impersonation — rogue device masquerades as a legitimate zone and accepts or intercepts commands
HPC crash without graceful shutdown — zone controllers remain active with no watchdog kicks, entering indeterminate state
Priority inversion — PriorityCritical command (e.g. emergency brake) blocked behind lower-priority commands under load
Command flooding — runaway HPC software loop overwhelms zone controller, causing it to drop safety-critical commands
Command replay — attacker retransmits a previously captured valid command in a new vehicle context, causing unintended actuation

safetyGoals.description: The RCP library shall detect and report command delivery failures within the timeout period.
The RCP library shall validate that commands are addressed to the correct zone and reject mismatched deliveries.
The RCP library shall provide a CmdWatchdog command type and ensure it is delivered with at least PriorityHigh.
Maximum end-to-end command latency shall be bounded and monitored; a violation shall be reported as a health event.
Command and response payloads shall be integrity-protected using a frame check sequence (CRC); frames failing the check shall be silently discarded.
Zone controller identity shall be authenticated before commands are accepted; an unauthenticated response shall be rejected.
Zone controllers shall detect cessation of watchdog kicks and enter a defined safe state within the configured watchdog timeout.
PriorityCritical commands shall never be delayed by Normal or High priority commands in any queue or admission-control mechanism.
The HPC shall enforce per-zone command rate limits; commands exceeding the rate limit shall be rejected with ErrBusy before reaching the zone controller.
Commands shall carry monotonically increasing sequence counters; frames whose counter falls outside the anti-replay window shall be rejected.
